title: Fundamentals of Critical Care Nursing
description: Comprehensive introduction to critical care nursing principles, covering patient assessment, hemodynamic monitoring, ventilator management, and multi-organ support. Designed for nurses transitioning into ICU settings.
modules: Overview of ICU structure, team roles, and ethical considerations in critical care nursing.
Understanding arterial lines, CVP monitoring, and cardiac output measurement.
Modes of mechanical ventilation, alarm management, and weaning protocols.
Sedation and Pain Management in ICU
Assessment scales, medication administration, and daily sedation interruptions.
Common ICU medications including vasopressors, inotropes, and antiarrhythmics.

title: Advanced Hemodynamic Monitoring
description: Deep dive into invasive and non-invasive hemodynamic monitoring techniques including PICCO, EVLW measurement, and fluid responsiveness assessment for critically ill patients.
modules: Cardiac physiology, preload, afterload, and contractility concepts.
Arterial lines, pulmonary artery catheters, and CVP interpretation.
Echocardiography basics, bioimpedance, and esophageal Doppler.
Passive leg raise, stroke volume variation, and pulse pressure variation.

title: Mechanical Ventilation Mastery
description: Complete guide to mechanical ventilation modes, settings optimization, troubleshooting alarms, and evidence-based weaning strategies for optimal patient outcomes.
modules: Lung mechanics, gas exchange, and the work of breathing.
Volume control, pressure control, SIMV, PSV, and high-frequency ventilation.
How to set up ventilation based on disease pathology and patient needs.
Systematic approach to common and critical ventilator alarms.
Spontaneous breathing trials, weaning protocols, and extubation readiness.
ARDS, COPD, obesity hypoventilation, and prone positioning.

title: Sepsis Management for Nurses
description: Evidence-based approach to early sepsis recognition, the Surviving Sepsis Campaign bundles, and nursing interventions that improve patient survival rates.
modules: Understanding the inflammatory cascade and organ dysfunction.
qSOFA, SIRS criteria, and lactate monitoring.
Fluid resuscitation, antibiotic timing, and vasopressor management.

title: Critical Care Nursing for Nigerian Hospitals
description: Context-specific critical care training addressing resource-limited ICU challenges in Nigerian hospitals, including equipment improvisation and staffing optimization.
modules: Equipment prioritization, space optimization, and infection control in Nigerian ICUs.
Clinical assessment techniques when monitoring equipment is unavailable.
Malaria complications, obstetric emergencies, and traumatic brain injury.
SBAR framework and shift handover best practices.

title: ECMO Nursing Care
description: Specialized training on Extracorporeal Membrane Oxygenation (ECMO) patient management, circuit monitoring, anticoagulation protocols, and complication recognition.
modules: Understanding VV-ECMO and VA-ECMO circuits and gas exchange.
Pump flow, sweep gas, and circuit pressure monitoring.
Heparin titration, ACT monitoring, and bleeding risk management.
Cannula issues, hemolysis, air embolism, and circuit thrombosis.

title: Neurocritical Care Nursing
description: Specialized course on neurological assessment, ICP monitoring, stroke care, TBI management, and neuroprotective strategies in the critical care environment.
modules: GCS, pupillary assessment, and neurological vital signs.
Intracranial pressure monitoring, EVD management, and cerebral perfusion.
Ischemic and hemorrhagic stroke management protocols.

title: Critical Care Pharmacology
description: In-depth study of medications commonly used in ICU settings including vasopressors, inotropes, sedatives, paralytics, and antiarrhythmics with dosing and monitoring protocols.
modules: Propofol, midazolam, fentanyl, and dexmedetomidine management.
Indications, monitoring, and reversal of paralytics.
Heparin, enoxaparin, and direct oral anticoagulants.

title: Post-Operative Critical Care
description: Nursing management of post-surgical patients in ICU, including cardiac surgery, major abdominal procedures, and transplant patient care.
modules: Systematic approach to the post-surgical ICU patient.
CABG, valve surgery, and post-cardiac surgery monitoring.
Post-operative fluid shifts, electrolyte abnormalities, and replacement.
Multimodal analgesia and regional anesthesia management.

title: ARDS and Acute Lung Injury
description: Comprehensive course on Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome pathophysiology, Berlin criteria, lung-protective ventilation, prone positioning, and rescue therapies.
modules: Berlin criteria, chest imaging, and differential diagnosis.
Low tidal volume strategy, PEEP titration, and driving pressure.
Indications, technique, contraindications, and nursing care during prone.
Neuromuscular blockade, inhaled pulmonary vasodilators, and ECMO referral.

title: Multi-Organ Dysfunction Syndrome
description: Understanding the pathophysiology, assessment, and nursing management of MODS, including renal replacement therapy, hepatic support, and end-of-life considerations.
modules: Systemic inflammatory response and sequential organ failure.
CRRT indications, modalities, and nursing management.
Liver support, encephalopathy grading, and coagulopathy.
Palliative care integration, family communication, and withdrawal of treatment.

title: Trauma Nursing in Critical Care
description: Advanced trauma life support principles adapted for nurses, including primary and secondary surveys, damage control resuscitation, and massive transfusion protocols.
modules: Airway, breathing, circulation, disability, and exposure assessment.
Damage control resuscitation and massive transfusion protocols.
GCS monitoring, ICP management, and neuroprotection.
Immobilization, neurogenic shock, and autonomic dysreflexia.
Parkland formula, wound care, and inhalation injury.

title: Cardiac Critical Care Nursing
description: Specialized training in cardiac ICU nursing, including post-cardiac surgery care, heart failure management, mechanical circulatory support, and cardiac arrhythmia monitoring.
modules: Coronary circulation, cardiac cycle, and electrophysiology.
ST-elevation, bundle branch blocks, and arrhythmia recognition.
CABG and valve surgery monitoring, chest tubes, and pacing.
Classification, medication optimization, and device therapy.

title: Critical Care Nutrition
description: Evidence-based approach to nutritional support in critically ill patients, including enteral and parenteral nutrition, metabolic monitoring, and special populations.
modules: Screening tools, energy expenditure, and protein requirements.
Feeding protocols, tube management, and intolerance assessment.
Obesity, renal failure, hepatic failure, and traumatic brain injury.

title: ICU Delirium and Sedation Management
description: Comprehensive course on ICU delirium prevention, identification using CAM-ICU, and evidence-based sedation strategies to improve patient outcomes.
modules: Light sedation, daily sedation interruption, and SAT protocols.
Sleep promotion, orientation, and family engagement.

title: Pediatric Critical Care Nursing
description: Specialized critical care nursing for pediatric patients, covering developmental considerations, equipment sizing, medication dosing, and family-centered care.
modules: Age-appropriate vital signs, PAT assessment, and early warning scores.
Weight-based calculations, Broselow tape, and equipment sizing.
Respiratory failure, sepsis, DKA, and status epilepticus.
Communication strategies, parental presence, and bereavement support.

title: Critical Care Transport Nursing
description: Training for nurses involved in inter-facility and intra-facility critical care transport, covering equipment, safety, and in-transit patient management.
modules: Altitude, acceleration, and temperature effects on patients.
Monitoring devices, medication pumps, and oxygen supply calculation.
Airway, ventilation, hemodynamics, and emergency interventions.
Crew resource management, handover protocols, and documentation.

title: Critical Care Nursing Certification Review (CCRN)
description: Comprehensive review course preparing nurses for the CCRN certification exam, covering all body systems, professional caring, and test-taking strategies.
modules: Heart failure, shock, arrhythmias, and hemodynamic monitoring.
ICP, stroke, seizures, and neuro assessment.
CRRT, hepatic failure, and GI bleeding.
DKA, SIADH, DIC, and transfusion therapy.
Ethics, advocacy, collaboration, and clinical inquiry.

title: Oxygen Therapy and Delivery Systems
description: Detailed course on oxygen delivery devices, flow rates, FiO2 estimation, humidification, and patient monitoring during supplemental oxygen therapy.
modules: Oxygen cascade, alveolar gas equation, and hypoxemia mechanisms.
Nasal cannula, simple mask, and rebreather mask.
Venturi mask, high-flow nasal cannula, and non-rebreather.
Humidifiers, SpO2 monitoring, and arterial blood gas interpretation.

title: Acute Kidney Injury in ICU
description: KDIGO classification, prevention strategies, renal replacement therapy modalities, and nursing management of AKI in critically ill patients.
modules: KDIGO criteria, RIFLE classification, and biomarkers.
Nephrotoxic medications, fluid optimization, and contrast nephropathy.
CRRT vs. intermittent HD, modalities, and circuit management.
Fluid balance, electrolyte monitoring, and medication adjustment.

title: Emergency Nursing Fundamentals
description: Core principles of emergency nursing including triage systems, rapid assessment, emergency procedures, and mass casualty management.
modules: ED workflow, team roles, and communication systems.
ESI, Manchester, and South African triage scales.
Chest pain, dyspnea, trauma, and altered consciousness.
MCI triage, incident command, and resource allocation.

title: Trauma Nursing Core Course (TNCC) Prep
description: Preparation course for the Trauma Nursing Core Course certification, covering systematic trauma assessment, intervention prioritization, and critical decision-making.
modules: ITLS primary and secondary survey methodology.
Trauma airway management, needle decompression, and chest tubes.
Tourniquets, wound packing, and massive transfusion.
Cervical spine clearance, GCS monitoring, and neuroprotection.
Pediatric, geriatric, and pregnant trauma patients.

title: Pediatric Emergency Nursing
description: Specialized emergency care for pediatric patients covering assessment, common emergencies, medication dosing, and family communication in the ED.
modules: Appearance, work of breathing, and circulation assessment.
Croup, bronchiolitis, febrile seizures, and dehydration.
Weight-based dosing, error prevention, and high-alert medications.
Communication, consent, and supporting families in crisis.

title: BLS Certification Course
description: American Heart Association-aligned Basic Life Support training for healthcare providers, covering CPR, AED use, and relief of choking.
modules: High-quality chest compressions, rate, depth, and recoil.
Automated external defibrillator use and safety.
Conscious and unconscious patient airway obstruction management.
Role assignment, communication, and quality improvement.

title: ACLS Certification Preparation
description: Comprehensive Advanced Cardiac Life Support preparation course covering algorithms, pharmacology, mega-code practice, and post-cardiac arrest care.
modules: Epinephrine, amiodarone, and adenosine dosing and indications.
Team leader and team member practice cases.
Targeted temperature management, hemodynamic optimization, and prognostication.
STEMI, NSTEMI, and unstable angina pathways.

title: PALS Certification Preparation
description: Pediatric Advanced Life Support preparation course covering pediatric assessment, respiratory emergencies, shock management, and cardiac arrest algorithms.
modules: Initial impression, primary and secondary assessments.
Upper and lower airway obstruction, respiratory failure management.
Hypovolemic, distributive, cardiogenic, and obstructive shock.
Pediatric BLS, VF/pVT, asystole, and PEA.

title: Emergency Nursing in Resource-Limited Settings
description: Practical guide to providing emergency nursing care in resource-constrained environments, common in developing countries and rural areas.
modules: Manual triage systems and clinical decision-making.
Malaria, typhoid, snake envenomation, and heat stroke.
Safe patient transfer when resources are limited.

title: Toxicology and Poisoning Emergencies
description: Recognition and management of common poisoning and toxic exposures, including organophosphates, carbon monoxide, drug overdose, and envenomation.
modules: Toxidrome recognition, decontamination, and enhanced elimination.
Carbon monoxide, organophosphates, and heavy metals.

title: Stroke Emergency Care
description: Rapid stroke recognition, NIH Stroke Scale assessment, thrombolytic therapy protocols, and stroke unit nursing care for improved outcomes.
modules: Early warning signs and activation of stroke protocols.
tPA administration protocols, contraindications, and monitoring.
Blood pressure management, swallowing assessment, and secondary prevention.

title: Mass Casualty Incident Management
description: Preparation and response training for mass casualty incidents, including triage systems, incident command, and disaster nursing protocols.
modules: Frameworks for nursing response in disaster situations.
START, JumpSTART, and SALT triage methodologies.
Healthcare ICS structure, roles, and communication.
Psychological first aid and staff wellbeing.

title: Emergency Airway Management
description: Skills and knowledge for emergency airway management including basic airway maneuvers, supraglottic devices, endotracheal intubation assistance, and surgical airway.
modules: Airway classification, predictors of difficulty, and anatomy review.
Head tilt-chin lift, jaw thrust, and OPA/NPA insertion.
LMA, i-gel, and other supraglottic airway management.
RSI preparation, equipment, and post-intubation management.

title: Wound Management in Emergency
description: Emergency wound assessment, cleaning, closure techniques, and aftercare instructions for common ED wound presentations.
modules: Classification, contamination grading, and neurovascular assessment.
Sutures, staples, tissue adhesives, and wound approximation.
Dressing, infection signs, tetanus prophylaxis, and referrals.

title: Emergency Cardiac Care
description: Nursing management of cardiac emergencies including acute coronary syndromes, heart failure exacerbation, hypertensive crises, and cardiac tamponade.
modules: STEMI, NSTEMI, and unstable angina recognition and management.
Acute decompensated heart failure and pulmonary edema.
Blood pressure management in acute neurological and cardiovascular crises.
BLS/ACLS integration and targeted temperature management.

title: Emergency Nursing Documentation
description: Best practices for ED documentation including triage notes, procedural documentation, medical-legal considerations, and electronic health record optimization.
modules: ESI assignment rationale, chief complaint, and vital signs.
Conscious sedation, wound repair, and procedure notes.
EMTALA, consent, and documentation for legal protection.
Templates, smart phrases, and efficient documentation workflows.

title: Burn Emergency Care
description: Initial assessment and management of burn injuries in the emergency department, including burn depth classification, fluid resuscitation, and transfer criteria.
modules: Depth, extent (rule of 9s), and inhalation injury assessment.
When to transfer to a burn center and safe transport.

title: Shock Assessment and Management
description: Comprehensive understanding of shock pathophysiology, classification, clinical presentation, and evidence-based management strategies.
modules: Cellular oxygen delivery, utilization, and compensatory mechanisms.
Hypovolemic, cardiogenic, distributive, and obstructive shock.
Vital signs, skin signs, urine output, and lactate monitoring.
Fluid resuscitation, vasopressors, and goal-directed therapy.

title: Emergency Nursing for Nigerian Healthcare
description: Context-specific emergency nursing training for Nigerian healthcare settings, addressing common presentations, resource challenges, and referral pathways.
modules: Malaria complications, road traffic injuries, and obstetric emergencies.
Adapting triage systems for local resource availability.
CPR, bleeding control, splinting, and basic airway management.
Indications for transfer, communication, and safe transport.

title: Environmental Emergencies
description: Management of environmental emergencies including heat stroke, hypothermia, drowning, lightning injury, and altitude sickness.
modules: Heat exhaustion, heat stroke, and cooling techniques.
Triage, cardiac effects, and multisystem management.

title: Emergency Psychiatric Care
description: Assessment and management of psychiatric emergencies including acute psychosis, suicidal ideation, substance abuse, and violence de-escalation techniques.
modules: Mental status examination, risk assessment, and safety evaluation.
Verbal and environmental strategies for agitated patients.
Intoxication, withdrawal syndromes, and Wernicke encephalopathy.
Screening tools, safety planning, and disposition.

title: Emergency Nursing Procedures
description: Step-by-step training on common emergency nursing procedures including IV access, arterial puncture, NG tube insertion, urinary catheterization, and splinting.
modules: Radial and femoral arterial puncture technique.
Indications, technique, and confirmation of placement.
Male and female catheterization techniques and troubleshooting.

title: Obstetric Emergencies for ED Nurses
description: Emergency management of obstetric complications including eclampsia, postpartum hemorrhage, shoulder dystocia, and emergency delivery.
modules: Quick assessment of pregnant patients and fetal monitoring basics.
Recognition, magnesium sulfate administration, and BP management.
Causes, quantitative blood loss, and intervention protocols.
Precipitous delivery management and neonatal resuscitation.

title: Geriatric Emergency Nursing
description: Specialized approach to emergency care of elderly patients, addressing atypical presentations, polypharmacy, delirium, falls, and elder abuse.
modules: Infection without fever, silent MI, and atypical abdominal emergencies.
Medication reconciliation, Beers criteria, and adverse effects.

title: Emergency Ultrasound for Nurses
description: Introduction to point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) for emergency nurses, covering FAST exam, cardiac views, and vascular access guidance.
modules: Basic principles, probe selection, and machine operation.
Focused assessment with sonography for trauma.
Ultrasound-guided peripheral and central line placement.

title: Triage and Patient Flow Optimization
description: Evidence-based triage systems and patient flow strategies to reduce wait times, improve outcomes, and enhance emergency department efficiency.
modules: ESI, Manchester, Canadian, and South African triage scales.
5-level triage decision tree and resource prediction.
Front-end loading, results pending, and vertical patient flow.
Door-to-provider time, LWBS rates, and length of stay.

title: Pediatric Nursing Fundamentals
description: Core principles of pediatric nursing covering growth and development, family-centered care, communication with children, and age-specific assessment techniques.
modules: Developmental milestones, age groups, and growth charts.
Age-appropriate vital signs, assessment techniques, and pain scales.
Principles, communication strategies, and parental involvement.
Weight-based dosing, administration routes, and error prevention.

title: Neonatal Care Essentials
description: Fundamental neonatal nursing care covering newborn assessment, thermoregulation, feeding, common conditions, and when to refer to specialist care.
modules: APGAR scoring, physical examination, and gestational age assessment.
Preventing hypothermia, skin-to-skin care, and incubator management.
Breastfeeding support, formula feeding, and nutritional requirements.
Jaundice, infection, respiratory distress, and hypoglycemia.

title: Childhood Immunization Programs
description: Comprehensive training on childhood immunization schedules, vaccine administration, cold chain management, and addressing parental concerns about vaccination.
modules: Nigeria NPI schedule, WHO EPI schedule, and catch-up vaccination.
Routes, sites, contraindications, and adverse event reporting.
Storage requirements, temperature monitoring, and vaccine handling.
Communication strategies and evidence-based responses to concerns.

title: Pediatric Pain Management
description: Evidence-based approaches to assessing and managing pain in children, including pharmacological and non-pharmacological interventions across developmental stages.
modules: FLACC, Wong-Baker, and numerical rating scales.
WHO analgesic ladder for children, dosing, and routes.
Distraction, guided imagery, and comfort measures.

title: Pediatric Respiratory Conditions
description: Nursing management of common pediatric respiratory conditions including asthma, bronchiolitis, pneumonia, and croup in various healthcare settings.
modules: Respiratory rate, work of breathing, and oxygen delivery.
Acute exacerbation, step-wise therapy, and action plans.
Assessment, supportive care, and when to escalate.

title: Pediatric Infectious Diseases
description: Nursing management of childhood infections including malaria, measles, HIV, tuberculosis, and neglected tropical diseases prevalent in African settings.
modules: Severe malaria, ACT treatment, and supportive care.
ART adherence, opportunistic infections, and disclosure.
Diagnosis challenges, treatment, and contact tracing.

title: Pediatric Sickle Cell Disease Care
description: Specialized nursing care for children with sickle cell disease, covering crisis management, hydroxyurea therapy, transfusion, and psychosocial support.
modules: Pain assessment, analgesia protocols, and hydration.
Coping strategies, school support, and transition to adult care.

title: Pediatric Fluid and Electrolyte Management
description: Calculation and administration of IV fluids in children, dehydration assessment, electrolyte imbalance correction, and monitoring strategies.
modules: WHO classification, clinical signs, and weight-based assessment.
Maintenance, deficit replacement, and ongoing losses.
Sodium, potassium, and calcium abnormalities in children.
Fluid balance charts, weight monitoring, and reassessment.

title: Child Development and Behavioral Health
description: Understanding normal child development, recognizing developmental delays, autism spectrum disorders, ADHD, and behavioral management strategies.
modules: Gross motor, fine motor, language, and social milestones.
M-CHAT, Ages and Stages, and Denver II.
Early signs, referral pathways, and supportive care.
Behavioral strategies, medication monitoring, and school support.

title: Pediatric Emergency Assessment
description: Systematic approach to the acutely ill child, pediatric early warning scores, recognition of deterioration, and escalation protocols.
modules: Appearance, work of breathing, and circulation evaluation.
Subtle signs, trending vitals, and communication frameworks.
Rapid response teams, communication, and documentation.

title: Pediatric Oncology Nursing Basics
description: Introduction to childhood cancers, chemotherapy administration, central line care, infection prevention, and supportive care for pediatric oncology patients.
modules: Leukemia, lymphoma, brain tumors, and solid tumors.
Safe handling, administration protocols, and extravasation management.
PICC, Broviac, and port management and infection prevention.
Nausea management, nutrition, pain, and psychosocial support.

title: Pediatric Surgical Nursing
description: Pre-operative and post-operative care of pediatric surgical patients, including common procedures, pain management, and family education.
modules: Fasting guidelines, pre-assessment, and family preparation.
Hernia repair, appendectomy, and orthopedic procedures.
Pain management, wound care, and discharge planning.
Teaching strategies, anxiety reduction, and follow-up.

title: Pediatric Nutritional Assessment
description: Assessment of nutritional status in children, management of malnutrition, WHO growth standards, and therapeutic feeding programs.
modules: WHO growth standards, MUAC, and dietary assessment.
Breastfeeding counseling, complementary feeding, and IYCF guidelines.

title: Pediatric Skin Conditions
description: Recognition and management of common pediatric skin conditions including eczema, infections, infestations, and dermatological emergencies.
modules: Stevens-Johnson syndrome, cellulitis, and purpura fulminans.
Burns, lacerations, and chronic wound management.

title: Adolescent Health Nursing
description: Health assessment and promotion for adolescents, covering reproductive health, mental health, substance abuse, eating disorders, and risk-taking behaviors.
modules: Contraception, STI screening, and pregnancy prevention.
Depression, anxiety, and suicide risk assessment.
Substance use, self-harm, and health promotion strategies.

title: Pediatric Diabetes Management
description: Nursing care of children with type 1 and type 2 diabetes, insulin management, glucose monitoring, diabetic ketoacidosis, and family education.
modules: Devices, injection techniques, and dose calculation.
Recognition, fluid management, and insulin infusion protocols.
Carb counting, sick day rules, and school management plans.

title: Pediatric Neurological Nursing
description: Nursing management of children with neurological conditions including seizures, cerebral palsy, neural tube defects, and neurodegenerative disorders.
modules: Age-appropriate assessment, GCS, and developmental reflexes.
Classification, emergency management, and antiepileptic medications.
Classification, spasticity management, and multidisciplinary approach.

title: Pediatric Cardiac Nursing
description: Care of children with congenital and acquired heart disease, including cyanotic and acyanotic defects, cardiac surgery recovery, and heart failure management.
modules: Tetralogy of Fallot, transposition, and truncus arteriosus.
Post-operative monitoring, chest tubes, and pacing wires.
Medication management, nutritional support, and transplant considerations.

title: School Health Nursing
description: Comprehensive guide to school nursing practice including health screening, chronic disease management, medication administration, and health education in schools.
modules: Screening schedules, health records, and policy development.
Asthma, diabetes, allergies, and epilepsy management plans.
Age-appropriate health topics, teaching strategies, and evaluation.

title: Pediatric Nephrology Nursing
description: Nursing care of children with kidney disorders including nephrotic syndrome, acute kidney injury, chronic kidney disease, and dialysis management.
modules: Urinalysis, GFR estimation, and blood pressure monitoring.
Steroid management, edema care, and relapse assessment.
Prevention, fluid management, and renal replacement therapy.
Growth monitoring, dietary management, and dialysis preparation.

title: Child Protection and Safeguarding
description: Recognizing signs of child abuse and neglect, documentation, reporting procedures, legal frameworks, and supporting affected children and families.
modules: Physical, emotional, sexual abuse indicators and neglect signs.
Accurate documentation, forensic considerations, and mandatory reporting.
Child rights acts, consent, and court processes.
Trauma-informed care, referral pathways, and multidisciplinary approach.

title: Pediatric Gastroenterology Nursing
description: Management of common pediatric GI conditions including diarrheal diseases, constipation, inflammatory bowel disease, and liver disorders.
modules: ORT, zinc supplementation, and danger sign recognition.
Crohn disease, ulcerative colitis, and nutritional support.
Neonatal jaundice, hepatitis, and liver failure.

title: Play Therapy and Distraction in Pediatric Care
description: Therapeutic play techniques and distraction strategies to reduce anxiety and pain in children during medical procedures and hospitalization.
modules: Developmental play patterns and therapeutic play principles.
Bubble blowing, guided imagery, and technology-based distraction.
Positive reinforcement, coping promotion, and family support.

title: Antenatal Care and Assessment
description: Comprehensive antenatal nursing care covering initial assessment, routine screening, risk identification, health promotion, and birth preparation.
modules: History taking, physical examination, and risk assessment.
Blood tests, ultrasound, and gestational diabetes screening.
Nutrition, exercise, substance avoidance, and mental health.
Birth planning, warning signs, and when to present to facility.

title: Emergency Obstetric and Newborn Care (EmONC)
description: Life-saving emergency obstetric and newborn care interventions including signal functions, hemorrhage management, eclampsia, and assisted delivery.
modules: Parenteral antibiotics, oxytocics, anticonvulsants, and manual removal.
Active management of third stage, uterotonics, and bimanual compression.
Magnesium sulfate protocol, blood pressure control, and monitoring.
Vacuum extraction and forceps indications and techniques.
HBB algorithm, ventilation, and chest compressions.

title: Normal Labor and Delivery Care
description: Evidence-based intrapartum care including labor assessment, partograph use, pain management, delivery technique, and immediate newborn care.
modules: Cervical dilation, station, and labor stage determination.
Delivery of head, shoulders, cord clamping, and placenta.

title: Postpartum Care and Complications
description: Comprehensive postpartum nursing care covering physiological changes, breastfeeding support, postpartum depression screening, and complication recognition.
modules: Involution, lochia, vital signs, and pain assessment.
Latch assessment, common problems, and expressing breastmilk.
Hemorrhage, infection, venous thromboembolism, and hypertension.
Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale and referral pathways.

title: Midwifery Practice in Nigeria
description: Context-specific midwifery training for Nigerian healthcare settings, aligned with NMCN standards, addressing local challenges and referral systems.
modules: NMCN regulations, scope of practice, and professional requirements.
Home visits, traditional birth attendant collaboration, and outreach.
Identification of high-risk pregnancies, communication, and transport.
Respecting traditions while promoting evidence-based practice.

title: Gestational Diabetes Management
description: Screening, diagnosis, and management of gestational diabetes mellitus, including glucose monitoring, dietary management, insulin therapy, and post-delivery follow-up.
modules: OGTT, risk factors, and diagnostic criteria.
Self-monitoring, target ranges, and pattern management.
Carb counting, meal planning, and nutritional counseling.

title: Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy
description: Comprehensive management of pre-eclampsia, eclampsia, HELLP syndrome, and chronic hypertension in pregnancy, including monitoring and emergency protocols.
modules: Gestational hypertension, pre-eclampsia, and eclampsia spectrum.
Blood pressure measurement, proteinuria, and laboratory monitoring.
Loading and maintenance doses, monitoring, and toxicity.
Induction of labor, corticosteroids, and postpartum management.

title: Obstetric Hemorrhage Management
description: Systematic approach to antepartum and postpartum hemorrhage, including causes, quantitative blood loss, medical and surgical interventions, and massive transfusion.
modules: Placenta previa, abruption, and vasa previa management.
4 Ts: tone, tissue, trauma, and thrombin.
Uterotonics, tranexamic acid, and fluid resuscitation.
Bakri balloon, B-Lynch suture, and peripartum hysterectomy.
Blood products, ratios, and coagulopathy management.

title: Breastfeeding Support and Lactation
description: Comprehensive lactation support training covering attachment, milk supply, common problems, expressing, and supporting mothers with breastfeeding challenges.
modules: Anatomy, hormones, and the let-down reflex.
Optimal latch techniques and positioning for effective feeding.
Engorgement, mastitis, nipple pain, and low milk supply.
Hand expression, pump use, and safe storage guidelines.

title: Fetal Monitoring and Surveillance
description: Interpretation of fetal heart rate patterns, cardiotocography, antenatal fetal surveillance, and appropriate escalation of concerns during labor.
modules: Fetal oxygenation, autonomic nervous system, and heart rate regulation.
Doppler and Pinard use, frequency, and documentation.
NICE and ACOG classification systems and escalation pathways.

title: Premature Labor and Birth
description: Management of preterm labor, tocolytic therapy, corticosteroid administration, magnesium sulfate for neuroprotection, and care of the preterm infant.
modules: Cervical assessment, fetal fibronectin, and risk scoring.
Betamethasone, dexamethasone, and magnesium sulfate protocols.
Delivery considerations, delayed cord clamping, and neonatal team activation.

title: Obstetric Anesthesia and Analgesia
description: Understanding pain relief options in labor, epidural management, spinal anesthesia for cesarean section, and nursing care of the anesthetized obstetric patient.
modules: Pain pathways, psychological factors, and cultural influences.
TENS, water immersion, breathing techniques, and doulas.
Monitoring, bolus dosing, and complication recognition.
Indications, rapid sequence induction, and MEND algorithm.

title: Cesarean Section Care
description: Pre-operative preparation, intra-operative nursing roles, and post-cesarean care including pain management, early mobilization, and breastfeeding support.
modules: Consent, blood grouping, antibiotic prophylaxis, and patient preparation.
Scrub, circulating, and patient safety roles.
Pain management, wound care, and vital sign monitoring.
Early initiation, positioning after surgery, and discharge planning.

title: Maternal Nutrition in Pregnancy
description: Nutritional requirements during pregnancy and lactation, micronutrient supplementation, dietary counseling, and managing nutritional challenges.
modules: Caloric needs, protein, iron, folate, and calcium requirements.
Iron, folic acid, calcium, and vitamin D guidelines.
Cultural dietary practices, food safety, and managing aversions.
Nausea, heartburn, gestational diabetes, and vegetarian diets.

title: Maternal Mental Health
description: Screening, assessment, and support for perinatal mental health conditions including depression, anxiety, PTSD, and psychosis.
modules: Prevalence, risk factors, and impact on mother and baby.
Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale and PHQ-9.
Recognition, supportive counseling, and medication considerations.

title: Maternal HIV Care
description: Prevention of mother-to-child transmission (PMTCT), ART management in pregnancy, infant prophylaxis, and supportive care for HIV-positive mothers.
modules: Testing, treatment, delivery, and follow-up protocols.
Recommended regimens, adherence support, and monitoring.
Mode of delivery decisions, infant ARV, and early infant diagnosis.
Disclosure, mental health, nutrition, and community support.

title: Multiple Pregnancy Care
description: Specialized nursing care for twin and higher-order pregnancies, including monitoring, nutritional requirements, complications, and delivery planning.
modules: Growth scans, Doppler studies, and complication screening.
Twin-twin transfusion, growth restriction, and preterm birth.
Timing, mode of delivery, and neonatal team preparation.

title: Obstetric Fistula Care
description: Prevention, recognition, and holistic care of obstetric fistula, including pre-operative and post-operative nursing care and community reintegration support.
modules: Causes, types, and impact on women.
Quality antenatal care, emergency referral, and community education.
Catheter management, wound care, and rehabilitation.
Counseling, skills training, and community reintegration programs.

title: Maternal Sepsis Management
description: Early recognition and management of sepsis in pregnancy and postpartum, including the WHO maternal sepsis criteria and bundle approach.
modules: WHO criteria, risk factors, and common sources.
Vital sign parameters, screening tools, and escalation.
Antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, source control, and monitoring.
Hygiene, antibiotic prophylaxis, and postnatal surveillance.

title: Stillbirth and Perinatal Loss Care
description: Sensitive and comprehensive care for families experiencing stillbirth or perinatal loss, including bereavement support, memory-making, and follow-up.
modules: Communication techniques and creating a supportive environment.
Photography, handprints, cultural practices, and naming.
Lactation suppression, physical recovery, and follow-up.
Grief reactions, support groups, and referral to counseling.

title: Family Planning and Contraception
description: Comprehensive training on contraceptive methods, counseling techniques, WHO medical eligibility criteria, and addressing misconceptions.
modules: Informed choice, shared decision-making, and method switching.
Category 1-4 classifications and common medical conditions.
Myths, side effects management, and community education.

title: Safe Abortion Care and Post-Abortion Nursing
description: Post-abortion care including complication management, contraceptive counseling, emotional support, and quality improvement in service delivery.
modules: Vital signs, bleeding assessment, and pain management.
Hemorrhage, infection, and retained products recognition.
Immediate post-abortion contraception and method selection.
Non-judgmental counseling and referral to mental health services.

title: Maternal Health in Francophone Africa
description: Formation sur la santé maternelle adaptée au contexte africain francophone, couvrant les soins prénatals, l'accouchement et les soins postnatals.
modules: Évaluation initiale, dépistage et promotion de la santé.
Surveillance du travail et aide à l'accouchement.

title: Water Birth and Hydrotherapy
description: Evidence-based training on water immersion for labor and birth, including pool setup, safety protocols, eligibility criteria, and neonatal considerations.
modules: Water temperature, hygiene, and emergency procedures.
Inclusion and exclusion criteria for water birth.
Cord management, breathing assessment, and thermoregulation.

title: Vaginal Birth After Cesarean (VBAC)
description: Nursing care and support for women attempting vaginal birth after cesarean section, including risk assessment, monitoring, and shared decision-making.
modules: Previous scar type, indication, and risk factors.
Presenting risks and benefits and supporting informed choice.
Fetal monitoring, scar assessment, and escalation triggers.
Rupture recognition, team preparation, and rapid response.

title: Community Health Nursing Fundamentals
description: Principles and practice of community health nursing, including population health assessment, health promotion, disease prevention, and community engagement strategies.
modules: Data collection, needs assessment, and priority setting.
Ottawa Charter, socio-ecological model, and behavior change theories.
Primary, secondary, and tertiary prevention strategies.
Stakeholder mapping, participatory approaches, and cultural sensitivity.

title: Primary Healthcare in Nigeria
description: Overview of Nigeria's primary healthcare system, PHC structure, national health policies, and the role of nurses in delivering essential primary care services.
modules: Ward health system, PHC centers, and referral networks.
National Health Act, NHIS, and minimum healthcare package.
Immunization, ANC, family planning, and childhood illnesses.
Leadership, task shifting, and community health worker supervision.

title: Epidemiology for Nurses
description: Introduction to epidemiological concepts, study designs, outbreak investigation, disease surveillance, and data interpretation for public health nursing practice.
modules: Incidence, prevalence, and measures of association.
Cohort, case-control, cross-sectional, and ecological studies.
Steps in investigating and controlling disease outbreaks.
IDSR, notifiable diseases, and reporting systems.

title: Health Education and Promotion
description: Designing, implementing, and evaluating health education programs, communication strategies, and behavior change interventions in community settings.
modules: Adult learning theory, cultural competence, and literacy.
Interpersonal, mass media, and social marketing approaches.
Health belief model, stages of change, and motivational interviewing.
Process, outcome, and impact evaluation methods.

title: Community Health Worker Supervision
description: Training nurses to effectively supervise and support community health workers, including training methods, supportive supervision, and quality improvement.
modules: Selection, training curriculum, and scope of practice.
Data quality, performance standards, and coaching.

title: Tuberculosis Control Programs
description: Nursing role in TB control including case detection, DOTS therapy, infection prevention, contact tracing, MDR-TB management, and community-based care.
modules: Symptom screening, sputum testing, and GeneXpert.
Directly observed treatment, regimens, and adherence support.
Second-line drugs, treatment monitoring, and infection control.

title: HIV/AIDS Community Care
description: Community-based HIV care including testing, ART adherence support, opportunistic infection prevention, stigma reduction, and differentiated service delivery.
modules: HTS approaches, self-testing, and index testing.
Differentiated service delivery, community refill groups, and viral load.
Cotrimoxazole prophylaxis, cryptococcal screening, and vaccination.
Community education, support groups, and rights-based approaches.

title: Malaria Prevention and Control
description: Comprehensive malaria control strategies including vector control, chemoprevention, case management, and community education for endemic areas.
modules: Transmission, life cycle, and endemicity classification.
LLINs, IRS, larval source management, and personal protection.
RDTs, ACT treatment, and severe malaria referral.
IPTp, IPTi, SMC, and mass drug administration.

title: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH)
description: Nursing role in WASH programs, including water quality testing, sanitation promotion, hygiene education, and WASH in healthcare facilities.
modules: Disease burden, transmission pathways, and impact on health outcomes.
Testing methods, household treatment, and safe storage.
Community-led total sanitation and latrine construction.
Minimum standards, infection prevention, and waste management.

title: Non-Communicable Disease Community Programs
description: Community-based approaches to NCD prevention, screening, and management including hypertension, diabetes, cancer screening, and health lifestyle promotion.
modules: Global and African NCD trends, modifiable risk factors.
Hypertension, diabetes, cervical cancer, and breast cancer screening.
Diet, physical activity, tobacco cessation, and alcohol reduction.
Medication adherence, follow-up systems, and self-management support.

title: Refugee and Displaced Population Health
description: Healthcare delivery in humanitarian settings, including rapid health assessment, communicable disease control, nutrition programs, and mental health support.
modules: Rapid assessment tools, mortality surveys, and priority setting.
Outbreak prevention, surveillance, and response in camps.
Therapeutic feeding, supplementation, and malnutrition screening.
IASC guidelines, psychological first aid, and referral pathways.

title: Community Mental Health Nursing
description: Mental health nursing in community settings, including crisis intervention, medication monitoring, psychoeducation, and integration of mental health into primary care.
modules: Mental status examination, risk assessment, and functional assessment.
Depot injections, adherence support, and side effect monitoring.
Illness management, relapse prevention, and family education.
Task-sharing, mhGAP, and collaborative care models.

title: Maternal and Child Health Community Programs
description: Community-based maternal and child health interventions, including home visits, growth monitoring, immunization outreach, and referral linkage.
modules: Antenatal, postnatal, and well-child visit schedules and content.
Measuring, plotting, and counseling on growth faltering.
Mobile clinics, tracking defaulters, and cold chain management.
Identifying danger signs, referral pathways, and follow-up.

title: Adolescent Health Programs
description: Designing and implementing adolescent-friendly health services, including SRH, mental health, substance abuse, and youth engagement strategies.
modules: WHO standards, service design, and provider training.
Contraception, STI management, and comprehensive sexuality education.
School-based interventions, peer support, and digital health.
Participatory approaches, peer educators, and advocacy.

title: Nutrition Programs in Community Health
description: Implementation of community nutrition programs including growth monitoring, micronutrient supplementation, therapeutic feeding, and food security assessment.
modules: Anthropometric surveys, dietary assessment, and food security.
Vitamin A, iron, zinc supplementation, and fortification.
OTP, SFP, and SC protocols and referral criteria.
Assessment, kitchen gardens, and income-generating activities.

title: Global Health Concepts for Nurses
description: Introduction to global health principles, health systems, SDGs, WHO frameworks, and the role of nursing in addressing global health challenges.
modules: Equity, determinants of health, and global burden of disease.
SDG 3 and interlinkages with other goals.
WHO building blocks, financing, and human resources for health.
WHO nursing framework, workforce issues, and leadership.

title: Community Health Data Management
description: Data collection, management, analysis, and use in community health programs, including HMIS, DHIS2, and quality improvement cycles.
modules: Surveys, registers, and routine data collection tools.
DHIS2, facility registers, and community data platforms.
PDSA, data-driven decision making, and feedback mechanisms.

title: Environmental Health for Community Nurses
description: Understanding environmental health hazards, climate change impacts, pollution effects, and community-level adaptation strategies.
modules: Air, water, soil pollution, and chemical exposures.
Heat stress, vector-borne diseases, and extreme weather.
Heat action plans, flood preparedness, and drought response.
Environmental health regulations and community advocacy.

title: Social Determinants of Health
description: Understanding how social, economic, and environmental factors impact health outcomes, and nursing strategies to address health inequities.
modules: WHO framework, Dahlgren-Whitehead model, and life course approach.
Education, income, housing, employment, and social support.
Measuring disparities, vulnerable populations, and intersectionality.
Screening for social needs, referral, and policy advocacy.

title: Community-Based Rehabilitation
description: WHO community-based rehabilitation matrix, including health, education, livelihood, social, and empowerment components for persons with disabilities.
modules: WHO CBR guidelines, inclusive development, and rights-based approach.
Rehabilitation, assistive devices, and chronic disease management.
Inclusive education, vocational training, and income generation.
Self-help groups, advocacy, and community participation.

title: One Health Approach for Nurses
description: Understanding the One Health approach connecting human, animal, and environmental health, and the nursing role in zoonotic disease prevention.
modules: Rabies, brucellosis, avian influenza, and Ebola.
Deforestation, wildlife trade, and emerging infections.
Surveillance, community education, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

title: Community Health Program Management
description: Planning, implementing, and managing community health programs, including needs assessment, resource allocation, monitoring, and evaluation.
modules: Situation analysis, logical framework, and work planning.
Budgeting, human resources, and supply chain management.
Indicators, data collection, and reporting frameworks.

title: Community Participatory Research
description: Participatory action research methods for community health, including community mapping, photovoice, and participatory data analysis.
modules: CBPR framework, ethics, and community engagement.
Asset mapping, GIS tools, and participatory techniques.
Community data interpretation, validation, and dissemination.

title: Infection Prevention and Control Fundamentals
description: Essential training on infection prevention and control practices, standard and transmission-based precautions, hand hygiene, and PPE use in healthcare settings.
modules: Pathogen, reservoir, portal of exit, mode of transmission, portal of entry, and susceptible host.
Hand hygiene, PPE, sharps safety, and respiratory hygiene.

title: Healthcare-Associated Infections Prevention
description: Strategies to prevent common HAIs including CLABSI, CAUTI, VAP, and SSI, with evidence-based bundles and surveillance methods.
modules: Central line insertion checklist, maintenance, and daily assessment.
Catheter indications, aseptic insertion, and removal protocols.
Head of bed elevation, oral care, sedation vacation, and weaning.
Antibiotic prophylaxis, skin preparation, and wound care.

title: Antimicrobial Stewardship for Nurses
description: Understanding antimicrobial resistance, the nurse's role in stewardship programs, culture collection, antibiotic timing, and de-escalation.
modules: Mechanisms, global burden, and drivers of resistance.
Culture before antibiotics, timely administration, and de-escalation prompts.
Blood cultures, urine cultures, and wound swab technique.
Antibiotic expectations, adherence, and resistance awareness.

title: Outbreak Management in Healthcare
description: Systematic approach to identifying, investigating, and managing outbreaks in healthcare facilities, including isolation, cohorting, and communication.
modules: Surveillance thresholds, cluster identification, and initial assessment.
Case definition, line listing, epidemiological curve, and hypothesis testing.
Isolation, cohorting, environmental cleaning, and visitor restrictions.
Internal communication, public health notification, and media management.

title: Tuberculosis Infection Control
description: TB infection control in healthcare settings, including administrative, environmental, and respiratory protection measures aligned with WHO guidelines.
modules: Administrative, environmental, and personal protection hierarchy.
Triage, cough etiquette, and TB IC plan development.
Ventilation, UVGI, and negative pressure rooms.
N95 fit testing, respirator use, and occupational health.

title: COVID-19 and Respiratory Pandemic Preparedness
description: Lessons from COVID-19 and preparedness for future respiratory pandemics, including PPE optimization, patient flow, and staff wellbeing.
modules: Key takeaways from COVID-19 for future preparedness.
Extended use, reuse protocols, and supply chain management.
Cohorting, triage systems, and surge capacity planning.
Psychological support, rotation systems, and burnout prevention.

title: Surgical Site Infection Prevention
description: Evidence-based strategies for preventing surgical site infections, including pre-operative preparation, intra-operative care, and post-operative wound management.
modules: Skin antisepsis, hair removal, and antibiotic prophylaxis timing.
Temperature management, glycemic control, and wound protection.
Dressing techniques, monitoring, and early detection of infection.
SSI surveillance methods, benchmarking, and feedback.

title: Needlestick Injury Prevention and Management
description: Prevention of sharps injuries, post-exposure prophylaxis protocols, reporting procedures, and occupational health management for bloodborne pathogen exposure.
modules: Engineering controls, work practice controls, and safety devices.
Immediate actions, risk assessment, and PEP protocols.
Incident reporting, occupational health referral, and counseling.
Safety culture, training, and device selection.

title: Environmental Cleaning and Disinfection
description: Best practices for environmental cleaning and disinfection in healthcare facilities, including cleaning agents, techniques, and monitoring.
modules: Difference between cleaning and disinfection, and product selection.
Terminal cleaning, daily cleaning, and outbreak cleaning protocols.
Fluorescent markers, ATP testing, and checklists.
Segregation, handling, and disposal of healthcare waste.

title: IPC in Resource-Limited Settings
description: Practical infection prevention and control strategies for healthcare facilities with limited resources, common in low- and middle-income countries.
modules: Minimum IPC requirements and stepwise improvement approach.
Water treatment, handwashing stations, and waste disposal alternatives.
Rational PPE use, alternatives, and making PPE last.
Leadership, training, and continuous improvement in challenging environments.

title: Bloodborne Pathogen Safety
description: Comprehensive training on bloodborne pathogen exposure risks, prevention, post-exposure management, and occupational health for healthcare workers.
modules: HIV, HBV, HCV transmission risks and prevalence in healthcare.
Standard precautions, safety devices, and work practice controls.
PEP for HIV, HBV vaccination, and follow-up protocols.
Vaccination, baseline testing, and confidential reporting.

title: IPC for Dental and Oral Health Settings
description: Infection prevention and control specific to dental and oral health settings, including instrument processing, waterline management, and aerosol mitigation.
modules: Standard precautions specific to dental settings.
Cleaning, sterilization, and storage of dental instruments.
High-volume evacuation, pre-procedural mouth rinse, and ventilation.
Biofilm prevention, flushing protocols, and testing.

title: Viral Hemorrhagic Fever IPC
description: Infection control for viral hemorrhagic fevers including Ebola, Marburg, and Lassa fever, with emphasis on PPE, isolation, and safe burial practices.
modules: Ebola, Marburg, Lassa fever, and CCHF virology and epidemiology.
Full PPE donning and doffing sequence and buddy system.
Red zone, green zone, and safe patient interactions.
Cultural sensitivity, safe body management, and community engagement.

title: Hand Hygiene Champion Training
description: Advanced training for hand hygiene champions, including WHO observation methodology, improvement strategies, and building a culture of compliance.
modules: Observation technique, data collection, and calculation methods.
Multimodal strategy, reminder systems, and feedback mechanisms.
Leadership engagement, role modeling, and positive reinforcement.
Ongoing monitoring, celebration, and addressing barriers.

title: IPC in Labor and Delivery
description: Infection prevention and control specific to maternity and delivery settings, addressing surgical site infection, neonatal infection, and healthcare worker protection.
modules: Unique challenges and standard precautions in L&D.
Maternal screening, intrapartum antibiotics, and newborn care.
Wound care, episiotomy care, and endometritis prevention.
Blood exposure prevention, PPE, and sharps safety.

title: IPC for Outpatient and Clinic Settings
description: Infection prevention and control in ambulatory care and outpatient settings, including waiting area management, triage, and point-of-care safety.
modules: High turnover, diverse patient population, and limited resources.
Respiratory hygiene, spacing, and cleaning schedules.
Injection safety, specimen handling, and equipment disinfection.
Orientation, ongoing education, and competency assessment.

title: Antibiotic Resistance and Global Health
description: Understanding the global antibiotic resistance crisis, surveillance systems, WHO GLASS, and the role of nurses in combating AMR.
modules: Drivers, burden, and projections for antimicrobial resistance.
Global surveillance systems, data reporting, and national action plans.
Antibiotic administration, culture collection, and patient education.
Human, animal, and environmental contributions to resistance.

title: Isolation Precautions and Room Management
description: Detailed guide on implementing isolation precautions, negative pressure room management, signage, and visitor policies in healthcare settings.
modules: Contact, droplet, airborne, and protective isolation.
Negative pressure, anteroom, and equipment placement.
Door signs, patient education, and team communication.
PPE for visitors, screening, and education.

title: IPC Program Development and Management
description: How to develop, implement, and manage a comprehensive IPC program in healthcare facilities, including committee structure, surveillance, and accreditation.
modules: Committee composition, terms of reference, and meeting schedules.
HAI surveillance, process indicators, and data analysis.
Writing IPC policies, procedures, and guidelines.
JCI, ISO, and national accreditation requirements.

title: Mental Health Nursing Fundamentals
description: Core principles of psychiatric and mental health nursing, therapeutic communication, mental status examination, and ethical considerations in mental health care.
modules: Verbal and nonverbal techniques, active listening, and boundaries.
Appearance, behavior, speech, mood, thought process, and cognition.
Involuntary admission, capacity, consent, and restraint use.
Recovery principles, strengths-based approach, and person-centered planning.

title: Depression and Anxiety Disorders
description: Assessment, nursing interventions, and management of major depressive disorder, generalized anxiety, panic disorder, and related conditions.
modules: PHQ-9, risk assessment, and psychosocial evaluation.
GAD-7, panic disorder screening, and functional impact.
Safety planning, psychoeducation, and supportive therapy.
Antidepressants, anxiolytics, and monitoring for side effects.

title: Substance Abuse and Addiction Nursing
description: Nursing care for patients with substance use disorders, including assessment, withdrawal management, harm reduction, and recovery support.
modules: Screening tools, AUDIT, DAST, and motivational interviewing.
CIWA protocol, seizure prevention, and delirium tremens.
MAT, naloxone distribution, and overdose prevention.
12-step programs, relapse prevention, and community resources.

title: Psychosis and Schizophrenia Care
description: Nursing management of psychotic disorders, including symptom assessment, medication management, relapse prevention, and psychosocial rehabilitation.
modules: Positive and negative symptoms, risk assessment, and mental state.
First and second generation, side effects, and monitoring.

Social skills training, supported employment, and family intervention.

title: Suicide Risk Assessment and Prevention
description: Evidence-based suicide risk assessment, safety planning, means restriction counseling, and postvention support for affected individuals and communities.
modules: Columbia Scale, warning signs, and risk formulation.
Collaborative safety plan development and means restriction.

Bereavement support, staff debriefing, and quality review.

title: Child and Adolescent Mental Health
description: Mental health assessment and intervention for children and adolescents, covering developmental considerations, common disorders, and family engagement.
modules: Age-appropriate assessment, brain development, and attachment.

title: Geriatric Mental Health Nursing
description: Mental health care for older adults, including dementia care, delirium management, late-life depression, and behavioral symptoms of dementia.
modules: Types of dementia, person-centered care, and communication.
NICE guidance, non-pharmacological approaches, and medication.

title: Psychiatric Emergency Management
description: Management of psychiatric emergencies including acute psychosis, severe agitation, self-harm, and involuntary admission procedures.
modules: De-escalation, environmental modification, and PRN medication.
Triage scales, risk assessment, and disposition planning.
Legal criteria, documentation, and patient rights.
Personal safety techniques, debriefing, and support systems.

title: PTSD and Trauma-Informed Care

modules: ACE study, neurobiology of trauma, and prevalence.

title: Eating Disorders Nursing Care
description: Assessment and management of anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, and binge eating disorder, including medical monitoring, nutritional rehabilitation, and therapeutic approaches.
modules: SCOFF, EDE-Q, and medical complications screening.

Meal planning, supplementation, and weight restoration.

title: Psychopharmacology for Nurses
description: Overview of psychiatric medications including antidepressants, antipsychotics, mood stabilizers, anxiolytics, and stimulants, with monitoring and side effect management.
modules: SSRIs, SNRIs, TCAs, and MAOIs mechanisms and monitoring.

Lithium, valproate, and carbamazepine levels and monitoring.

title: Mental Health in African Context
description: Mental health nursing adapted for African settings, addressing cultural beliefs, traditional healing integration, stigma reduction, and task-sharing approaches.
modules: Belief systems, explanatory models, and cultural formulation.
Referral pathways, boundary setting, and integrative approaches.
Community education, media engagement, and advocacy.
WHO mhGAP, primary care integration, and supervision models.

title: Forensic Psychiatric Nursing
description: Nursing care in forensic psychiatric settings, including risk assessment, managing violence, legal aspects, and rehabilitation of offenders with mental illness.
modules: Scope of practice, legal frameworks, and ethical dilemmas.

title: Perinatal Mental Health
description: Mental health assessment and support during pregnancy and postpartum, covering depression, anxiety, OCD, and psychosis in the perinatal period.
modules: EPDS, timing of screening, and risk factors.
Evidence-based treatments and medication in pregnancy/breastfeeding.

title: Group Therapy in Psychiatric Nursing
description: Facilitating therapeutic groups in mental health settings, including group dynamics, leadership styles, psychoeducation groups, and support groups.
modules: Forming, storming, norming, performing, and adjourning.

Co-facilitation, peer support models, and community groups.

title: Electroconvulsive Therapy Nursing
description: Nursing care before, during, and after ECT treatment, including patient preparation, monitoring, side effect management, and patient education.
modules: Mechanism of action, indications, and contraindications.
Fasting, consent, baseline assessment, and medication review.
Monitoring, seizure observation, and airway management.
Recovery monitoring, cognitive assessment, and follow-up.

title: Mental Health First Aid
description: Training in mental health first aid, providing initial help to someone developing a mental health problem or experiencing a mental health crisis.
modules: ALGEE framework: Approach, Listen, Give reassurance, Encourage professional help, Encourage self-help.
Recognizing signs, providing support, and connecting to help.
Grounding techniques, supportive communication, and resources.
Overdose recognition, harm reduction, and supportive response.

title: Psychiatric Rehabilitation and Recovery
description: Principles and practices of psychiatric rehabilitation, including skills training, supported employment, housing, and community integration for recovery.
modules: Personal recovery, hope, and self-determination principles.
Communication, daily living, and community integration skills.
IPS model, workplace accommodations, and job coaching.
Housing, social networks, and meaningful activities.

title: Dual Diagnosis: Mental Health and Substance Use
description: Integrated assessment and management of co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders, including screening, treatment planning, and relapse prevention.
modules: Integrated screening, chronological assessment, and formulation.
MI, CBT, and contingency management for co-occurring disorders.
Drug interactions, adherence, and monitoring in dual diagnosis.
Coping skills, support networks, and crisis planning.

title: Oncology Nursing Fundamentals
description: Core principles of cancer nursing, including cancer biology, staging, treatment modalities, symptom management, and psychosocial support across the cancer journey.
modules: Cell cycle, mutations, carcinogenesis, and metastasis.
TNM classification, grade, and treatment implications.
Surgery, chemotherapy, radiation, immunotherapy, and targeted therapy.
Communication, breaking bad news, and quality of life.

title: Chemotherapy Administration and Safety
description: Safe handling and administration of chemotherapy agents, including extravasation management, PPE use, and hazardous drug safety protocols.
modules: Hazardous drug handling, PPE, and closed system transfer devices.
IV push, infusion, and continuous pump administration.

Side effect education, lab monitoring, and emergency contacts.

title: Cancer Pain Management
description: Comprehensive approach to cancer pain assessment, WHO analgesic ladder, opioid management, adjuvant therapies, and interventional pain procedures.
modules: PQRST method, neuropathic vs nociceptive pain, and barriers.
Step-wise approach, opioid selection, and dosing.
Titration, conversion, rotation, and side effect management.
Anticonvulsants, antidepressants, nerve blocks, and palliative radiation.

title: Breast Cancer Nursing Care
description: Specialized nursing care for breast cancer patients, including screening, surgical options, reconstruction, lymphedema management, and survivorship.
modules: Prevention, early detection, and compression therapy.
Surveillance, late effects, and psychosocial support.

title: Palliative Care in Oncology
description: Integration of palliative care in cancer treatment, symptom management, advance care planning, and end-of-life care for oncology patients.
modules: WHO definition, early integration, and quality of life focus.
Nausea, dyspnea, delirium, and fatigue management.
Goals of care conversations, advance directives, and POLST.
Comfort measures, family support, and bereavement.

title: Immunotherapy and Targeted Therapy Nursing
description: Nursing management of patients receiving immunotherapy and targeted cancer therapies, including immune-related adverse events and patient education.
modules: Checkpoint inhibitors, CAR-T, and cancer vaccines.
TKIs, monoclonal antibodies, and specific toxicities.
Self-monitoring, when to report, and follow-up schedules.

title: Radiation Therapy Nursing
description: Nursing care during radiation therapy, including patient preparation, side effect management, skin care, and psychosocial support.
modules: Skin reactions, fatigue, mucositis, and esophagitis.
Fibrosis, secondary malignancies, and endocrine effects.

title: Cancer in African Populations
description: Oncology nursing adapted for African settings, addressing common cancers, late presentation, resource limitations, and culturally sensitive care approaches.
modules: Common cancers, late presentation, and limited treatment access.
HPV vaccination, VIA/VILI screening, and cryotherapy.
Treatment protocols adapted for limited-resource settings.
Traditional medicine, beliefs about cancer, and community education.

title: Oncology Emergencies
description: Recognition and management of oncological emergencies including tumor lysis syndrome, superior vena cava syndrome, spinal cord compression, and febrile neutropenia.
modules: Risk assessment, cultures, empiric antibiotics, and G-CSF.
Prevention, monitoring, and management of metabolic derangements.

title: Cancer Survivorship Care
description: Long-term follow-up and care of cancer survivors, including surveillance, late effects management, health promotion, and psychosocial wellbeing.
modules: Treatment summary, follow-up schedule, and surveillance.
Cardiotoxicity, neuropathy, cognitive changes, and secondary cancers.
Exercise, nutrition, smoking cessation, and weight management.
Fear of recurrence, relationships, and return to work.

title: Prostate Cancer Nursing Care
description: Nursing management of prostate cancer patients from screening through treatment, including surgery, radiation, hormonal therapy, and survivorship care.
modules: PSA, DRE, biopsy, and Gleason scoring.

Follow-up, PSA monitoring, and quality of life.

title: Pediatric Oncology for General Nurses
description: Essential knowledge for general nurses caring for children with cancer, including recognition, referral, safe handling, and family support.
modules: Warning signs, SIOP referral guidelines, and common presentations.
Basic safety principles for non-oncology settings.
Communication, practical support, and psychosocial care.
Long-term follow-up and transition to adult services.

title: Central Venous Access Device Management
description: Comprehensive management of CVADs including PICCs, tunneled catheters, and implanted ports, covering insertion assistance, maintenance, and complication management.
modules: PICC, tunneled, non-tunneled, and implanted ports.
Sterile technique, ultrasound guidance, and post-insertion care.
Heparin/saline locking, dressing changes, and blood draws.
Occlusion, infection, thrombosis, and air embolism.

title: Colorectal Cancer Nursing
description: Nursing care for colorectal cancer patients, including screening, surgical management, stoma care, chemotherapy, and survivorship.
modules: Anterior resection, APR, and stoma formation.
Appliance selection, skin care, and patient education.
Adjuvant therapy, CEA monitoring, and surveillance.

title: Nausea and Vomiting in Cancer Care
description: Evidence-based management of chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting, including emetogenic risk classification and antiemetic protocols.
modules: High, moderate, low, and minimal emetogenic chemotherapy.

title: Oncological Wound Care
description: Management of malignant wounds, tumor necrosis, and radiation skin damage, including odor control, exudate management, and comfort measures.
modules: Characteristics, odor grading, and exudate classification.
Metronidazole gel, charcoal dressings, and environmental measures.
Superabsorbent dressings, pouching, and skin protection.
Prevention, moisturizing, and management of wet desquamation.

title: Cancer Genetic Counseling Support
description: Nursing role in cancer genetics, including family history assessment, BRCA testing support, and genetic counseling referral facilitation.
modules: Inheritance patterns, penetrance, and common hereditary cancer syndromes.
Pedigree construction, red flags, and referral criteria.
Pre-test counseling, consent, and post-test support.
Family communication, decision-making, and surveillance options.

title: Bone Marrow Transplant Nursing
description: Nursing care for patients undergoing hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, including conditioning, infusion, engraftment monitoring, and complication management.
modules: Chemotherapy conditioning, stem cell infusion, and monitoring.
Neutropenia, mucositis, VOD, and infection prevention.

title: Head and Neck Cancer Nursing
description: Specialized nursing care for head and neck cancer patients, including airway management, nutritional support, speech and swallowing rehabilitation.
modules: Risk factors, staging, and treatment approaches.
Tracheostomy care, suctioning, and emergency protocols.
PEG tubes, enteral feeding, and swallowing rehabilitation.

title: Cervical Cancer Prevention and Care
description: Comprehensive course on cervical cancer prevention through screening and vaccination, and nursing care for women diagnosed with cervical cancer.
modules: Pap smear, HPV testing, and VIA/VILI in resource-limited settings.
